name: McCulloch-Pitts Artificial Neuron
description: Neurophysiologist Warren McCulloch and mathematician Walter Pitts published the first mathematical model of a neuron, describing how neural networks could perform logical computations. This paper is considered the foundational work of artificial neural network theory.

name: Alan Turing Proposes the Turing Test
description: Alan Turing publishes 'Computing Machinery and Intelligence,' proposing the Turing Test as a measure of machine intelligence — a foundational concept in AI.

sources.title: Alan Turing Proposes the Turing Test

name: Logic Theorist — First AI Program
description: Allen Newell, Herbert Simon, and Cliff Shaw at RAND Corporation created the Logic Theorist, the first program deliberately designed to mimic human problem-solving by proving mathematical theorems from Principia Mathematica. It successfully proved 38 of the first 52 theorems and is widely regarded as the first artificial intelligence program.

name: Artificial Intelligence Coined at Dartmouth
description: The term 'Artificial Intelligence' is coined at the Dartmouth Conference, organized by John McCarthy, Marvin Minsky, Nathaniel Rochester, and Claude Shannon. Widely considered the birth of AI as a field.

name: General Problem Solver (GPS)
description: Herbert Simon, Allen Newell, and Cliff Shaw developed the General Problem Solver, the first program to separate its domain knowledge from its problem-solving strategy, introducing means-ends analysis. It was an early and influential attempt at a universal AI reasoning engine.

name: Perceptron (Frank Rosenblatt)
description: Frank Rosenblatt at Cornell unveiled the Perceptron, the first trainable artificial neural network capable of supervised image classification learning. Demonstrated on an IBM 704 in July 1958, it was heralded as the first machine capable of learning to distinguish patterns without explicit programming.

name: LISP Programming Language
description: John McCarthy at MIT designed LISP (List Processing), the first programming language created specifically for artificial intelligence research. LISP introduced key concepts including garbage collection, first-class functions, and recursive definitions, and remained the dominant AI language for decades.

name: Arthur Samuel's Machine Learning Checkers Program
description: Arthur Samuel at IBM published his checkers-playing program, one of the world's first self-learning software systems, and coined the term 'machine learning.' The program improved its play through experience and demonstrated that computers could learn tasks they had not been explicitly programmed to perform.

sources.title: Arthur Samuel's Machine Learning Checkers Program

name: ADALINE and MADALINE Neural Networks
description: Bernard Widrow and Marcian Hoff at Stanford developed ADALINE (Adaptive Linear Neuron) and MADALINE, among the earliest practical neural network architectures. MADALINE was the first neural network applied to a real-world problem, using an adaptive filter to eliminate telephone line echoes.

name: DENDRAL — First Expert System
description: Edward Feigenbaum, Joshua Lederberg, Bruce Buchanan, and Carl Djerassi at Stanford began DENDRAL, widely considered the first expert system, designed to identify unknown organic molecules by analyzing mass spectrometry data.

name: Robinson's Resolution Principle for Automated Theorem Proving
description: J. Alan Robinson published the resolution principle, a complete method for mechanical theorem proving using unification. It became the dominant inference mechanism in AI and directly underpinned the development of logic programming and Prolog.

sources.title: Robinson's Resolution Principle for Automated Theorem Proving

name: ELIZA Chatbot Created
description: ELIZA, one of the earliest chatbots, is created by Joseph Weizenbaum at MIT. It simulated conversation by pattern matching, foreshadowing modern conversational AI.

name: ALPAC Report Halts Machine Translation Funding
description: The ALPAC report concluded that machine translation was slower, less accurate, and twice as expensive as human translation, leading the US government to drastically cut MT research funding. This was an early trigger of the first AI winter.

sources.title: ALPAC Report Halts Machine Translation Funding

name: Shakey the Robot
description: Shakey, the first general-purpose mobile robot capable of reasoning about its actions, is developed at Stanford Research Institute (SRI International).

name: Minsky & Papert's 'Perceptrons' Book
description: Marvin Minsky and Seymour Papert at MIT published Perceptrons, which formally demonstrated mathematical limitations of single-layer neural networks including their inability to compute the XOR function. The book significantly dampened enthusiasm and funding for neural network research for over a decade.

name: STRIPS Automated Planner
description: Richard Fikes and Nils Nilsson at SRI International developed STRIPS, the first major automated planning system that represented world states and goal conditions as logical formulae. STRIPS established the foundational action-language formalism that nearly all modern AI planning systems still build upon.

name: MYCIN Medical Expert System
description: Edward Shortliffe at Stanford developed MYCIN, a rule-based expert system that diagnosed bacterial blood infections and recommended antibiotic treatments using approximately 600 IF-THEN rules. MYCIN demonstrated that AI could match specialist physician performance.

name: Prolog Logic Programming Language
description: Alain Colmerauer and Philippe Roussel at the University of Aix-Marseille created Prolog, the first logic programming language, combining automated theorem proving with natural language processing goals. Prolog became the dominant logic programming language for AI research.

name: Lighthill Report Triggers First AI Winter
description: British mathematician Sir James Lighthill delivered a report concluding that AI research had failed to deliver on its promises. The report led the British government to cut AI funding at most universities, contributing to the first global AI winter (1974-1980).

sources.title: Lighthill Report Triggers First AI Winter

name: Werbos Discovers Backpropagation (PhD Thesis)
description: Paul Werbos at Harvard first described and implemented backpropagation of errors in his 1974 PhD dissertation, showing how to efficiently compute gradients for multilayer networks. Although largely overlooked at the time, this work predated the famous 1986 rediscovery.

name: R1/XCON Expert System at DEC
description: John McDermott at Carnegie Mellon developed R1 (later XCON) for Digital Equipment Corporation to automatically configure VAX computer systems. By 1986 it had processed 80,000 orders with 95-98% accuracy, saving DEC an estimated $25 million per year.

name: Japan's Fifth Generation Computer Project
description: The Japanese government launches the Fifth Generation Computer Project, a 10-year initiative to create computers using massively parallel processing and AI. It spurred global AI investment.

name: Hopfield Network
description: John Hopfield at Caltech published a landmark paper introducing Hopfield networks, a form of recurrent neural network with emergent content-addressable memory properties. The paper re-legitimized neural network research and helped spark the connectionist revival of the 1980s.

name: Rodney Brooks' Subsumption Architecture
description: Rodney Brooks at MIT proposed the subsumption architecture, a behavior-based approach to robot control that replaced traditional symbolic world-modeling with layered reactive behaviors. This paradigm shift led to practical mobile robots that could navigate real environments.

name: Backpropagation Rediscovery (Rumelhart, Hinton & Williams)
description: David Rumelhart, Geoffrey Hinton, and Ronald Williams published 'Learning representations by back-propagating errors' in Nature, popularizing backpropagation as a practical training algorithm for multilayer neural networks — establishing the algorithmic foundation for modern deep learning.

name: NETtalk — Neural Network Learns to Pronounce English
description: Terrence Sejnowski and Charles Rosenberg demonstrated NETtalk, a neural network trained by backpropagation that learned to convert written English text to phonetic speech. The system received widespread public attention and became a celebrated proof-of-concept for connectionist learning.

sources.title: NETtalk — Neural Network Learns to Pronounce English

name: Second AI Winter Begins
description: The market for specialized Lisp Machines and AI hardware collapsed as general-purpose desktop computers surpassed them in cost-effectiveness. Combined with the failure of rule-based expert systems to scale, this triggered the second AI winter (approximately 1987-1993).

name: CMU Sphinx — First Continuous Speech Recognizer
description: Kai-Fu Lee at Carnegie Mellon University unveiled Sphinx, the first high-performance speaker-independent continuous-speech recognition system based on hidden Markov models. Sphinx set the direction for all subsequent large-vocabulary speech recognition research.

sources.title: CMU Sphinx — First Continuous Speech Recognizer

name: TD-Gammon — Reinforcement Learning via Self-Play
description: Gerald Tesauro at IBM developed TD-Gammon, a neural network trained entirely through self-play using temporal-difference learning that reached near-championship level in backgammon — directly influencing later systems such as AlphaGo.

name: IBM Deep Blue Defeats Garry Kasparov
description: IBM's Deep Blue defeats world chess champion Garry Kasparov in a six-game match, marking the first time a computer beat a reigning world champion under standard chess rules.

sources.title: IBM Deep Blue Defeats Garry Kasparov

name: DARPA Grand Challenge 2004 — First Autonomous Vehicle Race
description: The first DARPA Grand Challenge was held in the Mojave Desert, offering $1 million to any autonomous vehicle completing a 142-mile desert course. No vehicle finished — the furthest traveled only 11.78 km — but the event galvanized the autonomous vehicle research community.

sources.title: DARPA Grand Challenge 2004 — First Autonomous Vehicle Race

name: Boston Dynamics BigDog — Quadruped Robot
description: Boston Dynamics, with DARPA funding, created BigDog, a dynamically stable quadruped robot capable of traversing rough terrain, running at 10 km/h, climbing steep slopes, and carrying up to 150 kg loads — a landmark in legged robotics.

name: DARPA Grand Challenge for Autonomous Vehicles
description: DARPA holds the Grand Challenge, a prize competition for autonomous vehicles that successfully navigate a 132-mile desert course. Stanford's Stanley wins, accelerating self-driving car development.

sources.title: DARPA Grand Challenge for Autonomous Vehicles

name: Netflix Prize — $1 Million Machine Learning Competition
description: Netflix launched a public competition offering $1 million to any team that could improve its movie recommendation algorithm by at least 10%, spurring major advances in collaborative filtering and ensemble methods.

sources.title: Netflix Prize — $1 Million Machine Learning Competition

name: DARPA Urban Challenge — Autonomous Vehicles Navigate City Traffic
description: Carnegie Mellon's 'Boss' won the DARPA Urban Challenge, becoming the first autonomous vehicle to safely navigate a 96-km urban course with intersections, traffic, and driving laws.

sources.title: DARPA Urban Challenge — Autonomous Vehicles Navigate City Traffic

name: Google Self-Driving Car Project Founded (proto-Waymo)
description: Google secretly founded the Self-Driving Car Project, led by Sebastian Thrun, tasking engineers to drive autonomously along ten 100-mile California routes without human intervention — completing the first route by December 2009.

name: ImageNet Dataset Published
description: Fei-Fei Li and collaborators at Stanford introduced ImageNet, a large-scale hierarchical image database with over 14 million annotated images spanning 20,000+ categories — the dataset that would fuel the deep learning revolution in computer vision.

name: Netflix Prize Won by BellKor's Pragmatic Chaos
description: After nearly three years of competition, the BellKor's Pragmatic Chaos team won the $1 million Netflix Prize by achieving a 10.06% improvement over Netflix's Cinematch algorithm, validating ensemble and matrix-factorization techniques.

sources.title: Netflix Prize Won by BellKor's Pragmatic Chaos

name: DeepMind Technologies Founded
description: Demis Hassabis, Shane Legg, and Mustafa Suleyman co-founded DeepMind in London, a neuroscience-inspired AI lab focused on building general-purpose learning algorithms — the company would become one of the world's most influential AI research organizations.

name: ImageNet Large Scale Visual Recognition Challenge (ILSVRC) Launched
description: The first ILSVRC was held, creating the benchmark that would define computer vision progress for nearly a decade. Teams competed to classify 1.2 million images across 1,000 categories.

sources.title: ImageNet Large Scale Visual Recognition Challenge (ILSVRC) Launched

name: Google Brain Project Founded
description: Google Fellow Jeff Dean, Greg Corrado, and Andrew Ng launched the Google Brain project, a deep learning research initiative using large-scale neural networks and distributed computing.

name: IBM Watson Wins Jeopardy!
description: IBM Watson defeats Jeopardy! champions Ken Jennings and Brad Rutter, demonstrating advanced natural language understanding and question-answering capabilities.

name: Siri Launches on iPhone 4S
description: Apple introduced Siri with the iPhone 4S, the first mainstream AI voice assistant integrated into a consumer smartphone, bringing natural language understanding and conversational AI to hundreds of millions of users.

name: Google's Deep Learning Image Recognition Breakthrough
description: Google's deep learning research team, led by Jeff Dean and Andrew Ng, trains a neural network on 10 million YouTube images that autonomously learns to recognize cats — demonstrating unsupervised feature learning at scale.

sources.title: Google's Deep Learning Image Recognition Breakthrough

name: AlexNet Wins ILSVRC 2012 — Deep Learning Breakthrough
description: Alex Krizhevsky, Ilya Sutskever, and Geoffrey Hinton's AlexNet won the ImageNet 2012 challenge with a top-5 error rate of 15.3% — nearly 10 percentage points better than the runner-up — proving that deep CNNs trained on GPUs could revolutionize computer vision.

sources.title: AlexNet Wins ILSVRC 2012 — Deep Learning Breakthrough

name: Word2Vec — Neural Word Embeddings
description: Google researchers Tomas Mikolov and colleagues introduced Word2Vec, computing dense vector representations of words that captured semantic relationships (e.g., 'king' - 'man' + 'woman' = 'queen'), transforming NLP.

name: DeepMind's Deep Q-Network (DQN) Plays Atari Games
description: DeepMind published research showing a single deep reinforcement learning agent could learn to play seven Atari 2600 games directly from raw pixels and scores, surpassing all prior methods on six games.

sources.title: DeepMind's Deep Q-Network (DQN) Plays Atari Games

name: Google Acquires DeepMind for ~$500 Million
description: Google acquired London-based DeepMind Technologies for a reported $400-650 million, one of the largest AI acquisitions at the time, granting Google access to world-leading reinforcement learning and deep learning research talent.

name: Dropout Regularization Technique Published
description: Srivastava, Hinton, and colleagues formally published the dropout technique, which randomly disables neurons during training to prevent overfitting, becoming one of the most widely used regularization methods in deep learning.

name: Generative Adversarial Networks (GANs) Introduced
description: Ian Goodfellow and colleagues introduced Generative Adversarial Networks, where two neural networks (generator and discriminator) compete to produce increasingly realistic synthetic data — opening an entirely new paradigm in generative AI.

name: Amazon Echo and Alexa Launch
description: Amazon introduced the Echo smart speaker powered by the Alexa voice assistant, the first always-on, voice-activated home AI device, pioneering the smart speaker category and bringing conversational AI into living rooms at scale.

name: Batch Normalization Introduced
description: Sergey Ioffe and Christian Szegedy at Google introduced Batch Normalization, a technique that normalizes layer inputs during training to dramatically accelerate deep network training — enabling 14x faster convergence.

name: DeepMind DQN Paper in Nature — Human-Level Atari Play
description: DeepMind published its landmark DQN paper in Nature, demonstrating an AI agent that achieved human-level performance across 49 Atari games learning solely from raw pixels and rewards.

sources.title: DeepMind DQN Paper in Nature — Human-Level Atari Play

name: Tesla Autopilot Launched
description: Tesla released Autopilot software version 7.0, the first commercial semi-autonomous highway driving system for consumer vehicles, enabling lane-keeping, adaptive cruise control, and automatic steering on public roads.

name: ResNet (Residual Networks) — 152-Layer Deep Learning
description: Microsoft Research's Kaiming He and team introduced Residual Networks (ResNet), using shortcut connections to train networks up to 152 layers deep, achieving 3.57% top-5 error on ImageNet and winning all five tracks of ILSVRC 2015.

name: OpenAI Announced
description: OpenAI publicly launches as a non-profit AI research company with the mission to ensure artificial general intelligence benefits all of humanity.

name: Google Cloud Vision API Public Beta
description: Google Cloud Vision API enters public beta, offering image understanding capabilities including label detection, OCR, and logo detection to developers and businesses.

sources.title: Google Cloud Vision API enters Beta, open to all to try!

name: Google Neural Machine Translation (GNMT) Deployed
description: Google launched Google Neural Machine Translation, replacing its phrase-based system with an end-to-end deep learning translation system for Google Translate, dramatically improving translation quality.

name: AlphaGo Zero: Mastering Go Without Human Knowledge
description: DeepMind published AlphaGo Zero in Nature, a version that learned entirely through self-play with no human game data, surpassing all previous versions within 40 days — demonstrating superhuman performance could be achieved tabula rasa.

sources.title: AlphaGo Zero: Mastering Go Without Human Knowledge

name: AlphaZero: Generalized Self-Play Across Chess, Shogi, and Go
description: DeepMind's AlphaZero achieved superhuman performance in chess, shogi, and Go within 24 hours of training from random play with no domain knowledge beyond the rules.

sources.title: AlphaZero: Generalized Self-Play Across Chess, Shogi, and Go

name: GPT-1
description: OpenAI releases GPT-1, the first Generative Pre-Training model for language understanding, establishing the transformer-based approach that would define the GPT series.

sources.title: Improving Language Understanding by Generative Pre-Training

name: Singapore Model AI Governance Framework (1st Edition)
description: Singapore releases the first edition of its Model AI Governance Framework, an accountability-based voluntary governance framework for AI.

name: GPT-2
description: OpenAI introduces GPT-2 with a staged release strategy due to concerns about misuse, marking one of the first major discussions about responsible AI release practices.

sources.title: Better language models and their implications

name: GPT-2: Too Dangerous to Release (Initially Withheld)
description: OpenAI announced GPT-2, a 1.5B parameter language model so capable at generating coherent text that the lab controversially withheld the full model, citing misuse concerns — sparking global debate on responsible AI disclosure.

sources.title: GPT-2: Too Dangerous to Release (Initially Withheld)

name: OpenAI Five Defeats Dota 2 World Champions
description: OpenAI Five became the first AI system to defeat reigning world champions in a major esports title, beating International 2018 champions OG in two back-to-back live matches of Dota 2.

sources.title: OpenAI Five Defeats Dota 2 World Champions

name: OECD AI Principles Adopted
description: The OECD adopts the first intergovernmental AI principles, establishing soft law guidelines for responsible AI development across member nations.

name: OpenAI–Microsoft Azure Exclusive Partnership
description: Microsoft becomes OpenAI's exclusive cloud provider with a $1B investment, establishing joint Azure AI supercomputing infrastructure.

sources.title: OpenAI forms exclusive computing partnership with Microsoft

name: AlphaStar Achieves StarCraft II Grandmaster Level
description: DeepMind's AlphaStar became the first AI to reach Grandmaster level in StarCraft II, surpassing 99.8% of active human players across all three playable races using multi-agent reinforcement learning.

sources.title: AlphaStar Achieves StarCraft II Grandmaster Level

name: GPT-2 Full Model Released (1.5B Parameters)
description: After a staged rollout spanning nine months, OpenAI released the complete 1.5 billion parameter GPT-2 model alongside detection research, concluding the incremental risk over smaller versions was acceptable.

name: Singapore Model AI Governance Framework (2nd Edition)
description: Singapore releases the updated second edition of its Model AI Governance Framework with expanded guidance and implementation examples.

sources.title: Second Edition of the Model AI Governance Framework

name: GPT-3
description: OpenAI releases GPT-3, demonstrating that large language models are powerful few-shot learners. Its 175 billion parameters represented a massive leap in scale and capability.

name: OpenAI API Announced
description: OpenAI publicly describes its API program, opening access to GPT-3 and establishing the commercial platform that would become central to the AI ecosystem.

name: Waymo Launches First Fully Driverless Public Robotaxi Service
description: Waymo became the first company to offer a fully driverless commercial ride-hailing service to the general public with no safety driver in the vehicle, launching Waymo One in Phoenix, Arizona.

sources.title: Waymo Launches First Fully Driverless Public Robotaxi Service

name: AlphaFold 2 Wins CASP14: Protein Folding Effectively Solved
description: DeepMind's AlphaFold 2 won CASP14 with a median global distance test score of 92.4, effectively solving the 50-year-old protein structure prediction problem with accuracy comparable to experimental lab methods.

sources.title: AlphaFold 2 Wins CASP14: Protein Folding Effectively Solved

name: Anthropic Founded
description: Anthropic is founded as an AI safety and research company (public benefit corporation), with a mission to build safe and reliable AI systems.

name: DALL-E (Original)
description: OpenAI introduces DALL-E, an early text-to-image generation model marking a significant milestone in multimodal AI capabilities.

name: CLIP: Connecting Text and Images via Contrastive Learning
description: OpenAI released CLIP (Contrastive Language-Image Pre-Training), a neural network trained on 400 million image-text pairs that matched ResNet-50's ImageNet accuracy in a zero-shot setting, enabling flexible visual understanding without task-specific training.

sources.title: CLIP: Connecting Text and Images via Contrastive Learning

name: EU AI Act Proposed
description: The European Commission proposes the EU Artificial Intelligence Act, a risk-based regulatory framework that would become the world's first comprehensive AI law.

name: Vertex AI Generally Available
description: Google Cloud launches Vertex AI, a unified ML platform for building, training, and deploying machine learning models including AutoML and MLOps tooling.

name: LaMDA Announced
description: Google introduces LaMDA, a dialogue-focused large language model, at Google I/O 2021, marking Google's entry into conversational AI models.

name: GitHub Copilot Technical Preview: AI Pair Programmer
description: GitHub and OpenAI launched the technical preview of GitHub Copilot, an AI code-completion tool powered by OpenAI Codex that suggests whole lines and functions in real time — the first broadly available AI programming assistant.

sources.title: GitHub Copilot Technical Preview: AI Pair Programmer

name: AlphaFold 2 Paper Published in Nature
description: DeepMind published the full AlphaFold 2 methodology in Nature, describing a novel architecture to predict protein structures with atomic accuracy, and released predictions for the human proteome.

name: OpenAI Codex: Natural Language to Code API Released
description: OpenAI released Codex via private API beta, a GPT-3-based model fine-tuned on billions of lines of code that could translate natural language to working programs — the engine behind GitHub Copilot.

sources.title: OpenAI Codex: Natural Language to Code API Released

name: Live Translate on Pixel 6
description: Google introduces Live Translate on Pixel 6, providing real-time on-device message translation and interpreter mode that works offline.

sources.title: More personal, more powerful: Meet Pixel 6 and Pixel 6 Pro

name: UNESCO Recommendation on the Ethics of AI
description: UNESCO Member States adopt the Recommendation on the Ethics of Artificial Intelligence, establishing a global ethical standard for AI development and use.

sources.title: Recommendation on the Ethics of Artificial Intelligence

name: China Algorithmic Recommendation Provisions Issued
description: China issues binding rules for algorithmic recommendation services, establishing one of the world's first comprehensive regulations targeting AI-driven content recommendation systems.

name: China Algorithmic Recommendation Provisions Take Effect
description: China's algorithmic recommendation rules officially take effect, requiring transparency and user control over algorithmic recommendation services.

name: PaLM Announced
description: Google announces the Pathways Language Model (PaLM), scaling to 540 billion parameters and demonstrating breakthrough performance across language tasks.

sources.title: Pathways Language Model (PaLM): Scaling to 540 Billion Parameters for Breakthrough Performance

name: DALL-E 2: Higher Resolution Text-to-Image with Diffusion
description: OpenAI announced DALL-E 2, generating photorealistic images at 4x the resolution of its predecessor using a diffusion model approach, enabling image editing, inpainting, and outpainting from natural language.

sources.title: DALL-E 2: Higher Resolution Text-to-Image with Diffusion

name: Anthropic Series B Funding
description: Anthropic announces its Series B funding round, a major early financing milestone to build safe, reliable AI systems and infrastructure.

sources.title: Anthropic Raises Series B to build steerable, interpretable, robust AI systems

name: OPT-175B Released
description: Meta shares OPT-175B (Open Pretrained Transformer) and related tooling, marking a major milestone in open large language model research.

sources.title: Democratizing access to large-scale language models with OPT-175B

name: Gato: DeepMind's Generalist Multi-Task AI Agent
description: DeepMind published Gato, a single transformer that can play Atari games, caption images, chat, navigate 3D environments, and control a robot arm — framing generalist agents as a viable path to AGI.

sources.title: Gato: DeepMind's Generalist Multi-Task AI Agent

name: Canada Bill C-27 (AIDA) Introduced
description: Canada introduces Bill C-27 which includes the Artificial Intelligence and Data Act (AIDA), a proposed national AI law.

name: LaMDA Sentience Controversy
description: Google engineer Blake Lemoine went public with claims that LaMDA had become sentient, prompting Google to place him on leave. The episode triggered worldwide debate about AI consciousness and anthropomorphism.

name: GitHub Copilot General Availability
description: GitHub launched Copilot as a generally available product, an AI pair programming tool powered by OpenAI Codex that suggests code completions in real-time. It became the first widely adopted AI coding assistant.

sources.title: GitHub Copilot is generally available to all developers

name: Midjourney Open Beta
description: Midjourney enters open beta, making its AI image generation tool available to the public via Discord and quickly becoming one of the most popular AI art platforms.

name: Stable Diffusion Public Release
description: Stability AI publicly released Stable Diffusion, an open-source text-to-image model. Its open nature allowed anyone to run it locally, sparking an explosion of AI art tools and community innovation.

name: HeyGen App Launch
description: HeyGen launches its AI avatar video creation platform, enabling users to generate professional videos with AI-powered virtual presenters.

name: Whisper Open-Source Speech Recognition
description: OpenAI releases Whisper, an open-source speech recognition system offering robust multilingual transcription capabilities.

name: EU AI Liability Directive Proposed
description: The EU proposes the AI Liability Directive (AILD), establishing procedural liability rules addressing proof and disclosure issues for AI-related tort claims.

name: Stability AI $101M Seed Round
description: Stability AI raises $101 million in seed funding, one of the largest seed rounds at the time, to expand its open-source AI model development.

name: China Deep Synthesis Provisions Issued
description: China issues binding deep synthesis (deepfake/synthetic media) rules requiring labeling, security assessments, and governance of AI-generated content.

sources.title: China: Provisions on Deep Synthesis Technology Enter into Effect

name: ChatGPT Launch
description: OpenAI launches ChatGPT as a research preview, a conversational AI interface that rapidly becomes the fastest-growing consumer application in history, fundamentally changing public perception of AI capabilities.

name: Perplexity Answer Engine Launch
description: Perplexity launches its AI-powered answer engine, offering a conversational search experience that combines LLM capabilities with real-time web search and cited sources.

name: Constitutional AI Paper Published
description: Anthropic publishes the Constitutional AI paper, introducing a novel approach for training AI assistants to be harmless via AI feedback rather than human labeling.

sources.title: Constitutional AI: Harmlessness from AI Feedback

name: ElevenLabs AI Voice Platform Launch
description: ElevenLabs launches its first human-like AI voice synthesis model, offering realistic text-to-speech and voice cloning capabilities that set a new standard for AI-generated audio.

name: Cursor AI Code Editor Launch
description: Cursor launches as an AI-native code editor forked from VS Code, integrating large language models directly into the development workflow for code generation and editing.

name: China Deep Synthesis Provisions Take Effect
description: China's deep synthesis provisions take effect, requiring providers of AI-generated synthetic media to implement labeling, security, and governance measures.

sources.title: China: Provisions on Deep Synthesis Technology Enter into Effect

name: OpenAI–Microsoft Multibillion-Dollar Partnership Extension
description: Microsoft and OpenAI extend their partnership with a multiyear, multibillion-dollar investment to scale Azure infrastructure for ChatGPT-era growth and model training.

name: Anthropic–Google Cloud Partnership
description: Anthropic selects Google Cloud as preferred cloud provider, with Google committing TPU/GPU clusters for model training and deployment.

sources.title: Anthropic Forges Partnership With Google Cloud

name: Replit Ghostwriter Chat
description: Replit launches Ghostwriter Chat, bringing conversational AI coding assistance to its online IDE, enabling developers to generate, debug, and explain code through natural language.

name: LLaMA (Llama 1) Announced
description: Meta announces the first Llama family of large language models, initially with gated research access, establishing Meta as a major open-model player.

sources.title: Introducing LLaMA: A foundational, 65-billion-parameter large language model

name: Claude and Claude Instant Introduced
description: Anthropic announces Claude and Claude Instant, its first commercially available AI assistant models, moving beyond closed alpha to broader availability.

name: GPT-4 Released
description: OpenAI releases GPT-4, a large multimodal model accepting image and text inputs, representing a significant leap in reasoning and problem-solving capabilities.

name: Google Bard Early Access
description: Google opens early access to Bard, its consumer AI chatbot, beginning the public rollout that would later be rebranded to Gemini.

sources.title: Google AI updates: Bard and new AI features in Search

name: Adobe Firefly Public Beta
description: Adobe launches the Firefly generative AI public beta, offering commercially safe AI image generation trained on licensed content, integrated into Creative Cloud workflows.

sources.title: Adobe Unveils Firefly, a Family of New Creative Generative AI

name: UK AI Regulation Pro-Innovation White Paper
description: The UK publishes its 'AI regulation: a pro-innovation approach' white paper, outlining a regulator-led principles-based approach rather than a single comprehensive AI statute.

name: Segment Anything (SAM) Released
description: Meta releases Segment Anything Model (SAM), a foundation model for image segmentation along with a large dataset, enabling promptable segmentation of any object in images.

name: Inflection AI Launches Pi
description: Inflection AI launches Pi (Personal Intelligence), a conversational AI assistant designed for empathetic, supportive dialogue rather than task completion.

name: Project Tailwind (NotebookLM Prototype) Revealed
description: Google reveals Project Tailwind at I/O, an AI notebook concept that learns from uploaded documents, which would become NotebookLM.

sources.title: With Project Tailwind, Google aims to make note-taking smarter

name: Oracle–Cohere Enterprise AI Partnership
description: Cohere models integrated into Oracle Cloud Infrastructure to deliver secure generative AI services for enterprise customers.

sources.title: Oracle to Deliver Powerful and Secure Generative AI Service

name: Function Calling and API Updates
description: OpenAI releases function calling capabilities and other API updates, enabling developers to build more structured and capable AI applications.

sources.title: Function calling and other API updates

name: NYC Local Law 144 AEDT Enforcement Begins
description: New York City begins enforcement of Local Law 144, requiring bias audits and notices for automated employment decision tools (AEDTs).

name: Claude 2 and claude.ai Public Beta
description: Anthropic launches Claude 2 alongside the public-facing claude.ai beta interface, significantly expanding access to Claude's capabilities.

name: NotebookLM Introduced
description: Google launches NotebookLM as a Google Labs product, an AI-first notebook grounded in user-uploaded documents for summaries, synthesis, and Q&A.

name: xAI Publicly Announced
description: Elon Musk publicly announces xAI, a new artificial intelligence company focused on understanding the true nature of the universe, positioning it as a competitor to OpenAI and Google.

name: Llama 2 Released
description: Meta and Microsoft announce Llama 2 with chat-tuned variants under a more permissive license than Llama 1, enabling both research and commercial use of open-weight models.

sources.title: Meta and Microsoft Introduce the Next Generation of Llama

name: MakerSuite Expands to 179 Countries
description: Google expands MakerSuite, its browser-based prompt prototyping tool and precursor to Google AI Studio, to 179 countries and territories.

sources.title: MakerSuite expands to 179 countries and territories

title: OpenAI acquires Global Illumination
description: Team acqui-hire; creative tools and infrastructure builders (ex-Instagram, Facebook, Pixar). Entire team joined OpenAI to work on ChatGPT core products.

name: Code Llama Released
description: Meta releases Code Llama, code-specialized variants of Llama 2 for code generation and discussion, available for research and commercial use.

sources.title: Introducing Code Llama, an AI Tool for Coding

name: SynthID for AI-Generated Images (Beta)
description: Google DeepMind introduces SynthID, an imperceptible watermarking and detection system for AI-generated images, advancing AI safety and content authenticity.

name: Claude Pro Plan Launched
description: Anthropic introduces Claude Pro, a paid subscription plan for claude.ai, initially available in the US and UK.

name: Anthropic–Amazon $4B Investment Partnership
description: Amazon invests up to $4B in Anthropic, with AWS becoming Anthropic's primary cloud and training partner. Claude becomes available via Amazon Bedrock.

sources.title: Amazon to invest up to $4 billion in Anthropic

name: GPT-4V (Vision) and ChatGPT Voice/Image Launch
description: OpenAI released GPT-4V, enabling GPT-4 to analyze and respond to image inputs, and simultaneously rolled out voice and image capabilities to ChatGPT Plus users — a major shift toward multimodal AI assistants.

name: Meta AI Assistant and AI Characters Announced
description: Meta introduces its AI assistant across WhatsApp, Messenger, and Instagram, alongside AI characters and Emu-based creative tools including image generation and AI stickers.

sources.title: Introducing New AI Experiences Across Our Family of Apps and Devices

name: Mistral 7B Released
description: Mistral AI releases Mistral 7B, its first open-weight model that outperforms larger competitors, establishing Mistral as a leading European AI company and champion of open-source AI.

name: DALL-E 3 Released in ChatGPT
description: OpenAI released DALL-E 3, its most capable image generation model, integrated natively into ChatGPT with significantly improved prompt adherence, typographic accuracy, and safety.

name: Grok Announced
description: xAI announces Grok, its first flagship AI chatbot and model family, initially available to X Premium+ subscribers with real-time access to X platform data.

name: GPT-4 Turbo + Assistants API + DALL-E 3 API
description: At DevDay, OpenAI announces GPT-4 Turbo, Assistants API (with Retrieval and Code Interpreter), JSON mode, seed control, and DALL-E 3 API access.

sources.title: New models and developer products announced at DevDay

name: GPTs (Custom ChatGPTs) Introduced
description: OpenAI introduces GPTs, allowing users to create custom versions of ChatGPT for specific purposes, democratizing AI application building.

name: Emu Video and Emu Edit (Research)
description: Meta publishes Emu Video (text-to-video) and Emu Edit (instruction-based image editing) research, advancing generative media capabilities.

sources.title: Emu Video and Emu Edit: Our latest generative AI research

name: Claude 2.1 Released
description: Anthropic releases Claude 2.1 with 200K context window, improved reliability, and beta tool use capabilities.

name: Pika 1.0 Released
description: Pika Labs releases Pika 1.0, its first major public AI video generation platform, enabling users to create and edit videos from text, images, and other videos.

sources.title: Pika, which is building AI tools to generate and edit videos, raises $55M

name: Gemini 1.0 Introduced (Ultra/Pro/Nano)
description: Google introduces the Gemini era with the Gemini 1.0 family of multimodal foundation models in Ultra, Pro, and Nano sizes.

sources.title: Introducing Gemini: our largest and most capable AI model

name: Mixtral 8x7B Released
description: Mistral AI releases Mixtral 8x7B with open weights, a sparse mixture-of-experts model that matches or exceeds GPT-3.5 performance while being far more efficient, advancing the open-source AI movement.

name: Tesla Optimus Gen 2 Reveal
description: Tesla unveiled Optimus Gen 2, a redesigned humanoid robot with new actuators, 10 kg weight reduction, 30% faster walking, and significantly more dexterous hands capable of handling delicate objects.

name: Gemini API + Google AI Studio Available
description: Google launches the Gemini API and Google AI Studio (evolved from MakerSuite), providing developer access to Gemini Pro for prototyping and building AI applications.

name: Imagen 2 GA on Vertex AI
description: Google makes Imagen 2 generally available on Vertex AI, providing enterprise access to its text-to-image generation capabilities.

sources.title: Imagen 2 on Vertex AI is now generally available

name: OpenAI–Axel Springer Media Licensing Deal
description: OpenAI licenses Axel Springer publisher content for ChatGPT with attribution, setting an early precedent for AI media licensing partnerships.

name: Beta Messages API and Commercial Updates
description: Anthropic introduces the Beta Messages API for a streamlined developer experience, along with expanded commercial terms and copyright indemnity changes.

sources.title: Expanded legal protections and improvements to our API

name: Suno AI Music Platform Launch
description: Suno launches its AI music generation web app out of stealth, enabling users to create original songs with vocals and instrumentation from text prompts.

sources.title: Exclusive: Gen AI music app Suno comes out of stealth

name: AlphaGeometry: Olympiad-Level AI Geometry Solver
description: Google DeepMind's AlphaGeometry solved 25 out of 30 International Mathematical Olympiad geometry problems — approaching gold-medalist level — combining a neural language model with a symbolic deduction engine.

name: Bard Rebranded to Gemini + Gemini App
description: Google rebrands Bard to Gemini, making it the primary consumer AI assistant experience, and introduces Gemini Advanced with Ultra 1.0.

sources.title: Bard becomes Gemini: Try Ultra 1.0 and a new mobile app

name: Sora (Research Preview)
description: OpenAI introduces Sora, a video generation model described as understanding world simulators, marking a major breakthrough in AI-generated video.

sources.title: Video generation models as world simulators

name: Gemini 1.5 (Early Access)
description: Google begins rolling out Gemini 1.5 with major long-context and multimodal upgrades in the Gemini model line.

name: Microsoft–Mistral AI Azure Partnership
description: Microsoft partners with Mistral AI to distribute Mistral Large and other models via Azure, taking a minority stake in the company.

sources.title: Microsoft and Mistral AI announce new partnership

name: Figure AI-OpenAI Collaboration and $675M Funding
description: Humanoid robotics startup Figure AI announced a $675M Series B and a collaboration with OpenAI to develop next-generation AI models for humanoid robots, signaling a convergence of frontier AI with embodied robotics.

name: Claude 3 Family (Haiku, Sonnet, Opus)
description: Anthropic introduces the Claude 3 family with three models: Haiku (fast/affordable), Sonnet (balanced), and Opus (most capable), representing a major generational leap.

sources.title: Introducing the next generation of Claude

name: EU AI Act Adopted by Parliament
description: The European Parliament adopts the EU AI Act, the world's first comprehensive AI regulation, establishing a risk-based framework for AI systems.

sources.title: Artificial Intelligence Act: MEPs adopt landmark law

name: Utah AI Consumer Protection Act (SB149) Signed
description: Utah signs SB149 'Artificial Intelligence Amendments', enacting AI-focused consumer protection disclosures including chatbot/AI disclosure requirements.

title: Apple acquires DarwinAI
description: Canadian AI startup specializing in making AI models smaller and more efficient for on-device use. Bolstered Apple's on-device AI strategy ahead of Apple Intelligence launch.

name: India AI Advisory Revised/Softened
description: India revises its AI advisory after industry concern, removing the mandate requiring AI models to seek government approval before deployment.

sources.title: Govt withdraws mandate requiring AI models to seek approval

name: NVIDIA Blackwell GPU Architecture Announced
description: NVIDIA unveiled the Blackwell GPU architecture at GTC 2024, promising up to 25x lower cost and energy for running trillion-parameter AI models compared to Hopper (H100) generation.

name: Microsoft Hires Inflection AI Leadership
description: Microsoft hired Inflection AI co-founder Mustafa Suleyman and most of the technical team to lead its consumer AI division (Microsoft AI), effectively ending Inflection's consumer Pi product.

sources.title: Mustafa Suleyman joins Microsoft to lead Copilot

title: Microsoft acqui-hires Inflection AI team

name: Suno v3 Released
description: Suno releases v3, a major quality and capability upgrade enabling full-length, radio-quality songs with improved vocals, instrumentation, and longer duration outputs.

name: Amazon–Anthropic $4B Investment Completed
description: Amazon formalizes the completion of its $4B investment in Anthropic, deepening Claude distribution across AWS services.

sources.title: Amazon and Anthropic deepen their shared commitment

name: Meta Llama 3 Released (8B, 70B)
description: Meta releases the Llama 3 family with 8B and 70B parameter models, representing a major upgrade in open-weight model capabilities.

name: Meta AI Built with Llama 3
description: Meta positions Meta AI as its flagship assistant experience built on Llama 3, integrated across Meta's family of apps.

sources.title: Meet Your New Assistant: Meta AI, Built With Llama 3

name: GPT-3.5 Turbo + Whisper APIs
description: OpenAI announces API availability updates for GPT-3.5 Turbo and Whisper, clarifying the ChatGPT API naming and positioning.

sources.title: Introducing APIs for GPT-3.5 Turbo and Whisper

name: OpenAI–Financial Times Content Partnership
description: OpenAI licenses Financial Times journalism for ChatGPT with attribution, enabling product collaboration and improved model responses.

sources.title: FT announces strategic partnership with OpenAI

name: Claude Team Plan and iOS App
description: Anthropic launches the Claude Team plan for organizations and releases the Claude iOS app, expanding access across platforms.

sources.title: Introducing the Claude Team plan and iOS app

name: Utah AI Consumer Protection (SB149) Takes Effect
description: Utah's AI consumer protection amendments take effect, requiring disclosure when consumers interact with AI systems.

name: GPT-4o (Omni) Released
description: OpenAI releases GPT-4o, a real-time multimodal model handling text, vision, and audio natively, significantly advancing AI interaction capabilities.

name: Claude Available in Europe
description: Anthropic expands Claude availability to Europe, including Pro and Team pricing in local currencies.

sources.title: Claude is now available in Europe

name: Veo Announced (Text-to-Video)
description: Google DeepMind introduces Veo, a high-quality text-to-video generation model, expanding generative AI into video creation.

name: Imagen 3 Unveiled
description: Google announces Imagen 3, its next-generation text-to-image model with improved quality and capabilities.

name: VideoFX, ImageFX, MusicFX Updates + Ask Photos
description: Google Labs expands creative AI experiments with VideoFX and updates to ImageFX and MusicFX, plus announces Ask Photos for Gemini-powered photo search.

sources.title: Introducing VideoFX, plus new features for ImageFX and MusicFX

name: SynthID Expands to Text and Video Watermarking
description: Google DeepMind expands SynthID beyond images to watermark AI-generated text and video, with broader product integrations.

sources.title: Watermarking AI-generated text and video with SynthID

name: OpenAI–Reddit Data Partnership
description: OpenAI gains access to Reddit's Data API for incorporating content into products. Reddit uses OpenAI models for new AI features.

name: Colorado AI Consumer Protection Act (SB24-205) Signed
description: Colorado enacts SB24-205, a comprehensive 'high-risk AI' consumer protection law focused on preventing algorithmic discrimination.

sources.title: SB24-205 Consumer Protections for Artificial Intelligence

name: EU AI Act Adopted by Council
description: The EU Council formally adopts the AI Act, completing the legislative process for the world's first comprehensive AI regulation.

name: OpenAI–News Corp Multi-Year Licensing Deal
description: Multi-year agreement for OpenAI to use News Corp content across its mastheads to improve AI products.

sources.title: News Corp and OpenAI Sign Landmark Partnership

name: OpenAI–Vox Media and The Atlantic Partnerships
description: OpenAI signs content and product partnerships with Vox Media and The Atlantic for attributed use of publisher content.

sources.title: OpenAI signs content deals with The Atlantic and Vox Media

name: Apple Intelligence Announced
description: Apple announces Apple Intelligence at WWDC 2024, its personal intelligence system integrating AI capabilities across iPhone, iPad, and Mac with on-device processing and a partnership with OpenAI.

sources.title: Introducing Apple Intelligence for iPhone, iPad, and Mac

name: Apple–OpenAI ChatGPT Integration
description: ChatGPT integrated into Apple Intelligence across iOS, iPadOS, and macOS, including Siri handoff with user consent.

name: Stable Diffusion 3 Medium Open Release
description: Stability AI released Stable Diffusion 3 Medium with a new Multimodal Diffusion Transformer (MMDiT) architecture that dramatically improved typography, prompt adherence, and photorealism.

name: Runway Gen-3 Alpha
description: Runway announces Gen-3 Alpha, a major leap in AI video generation quality offering improved fidelity, consistency, and motion for text-to-video and image-to-video creation.

name: Claude 3.5 Sonnet Released
description: Anthropic releases Claude 3.5 Sonnet, the first model in the Claude 3.5 family, setting new benchmarks for balanced performance and speed.

title: OpenAI acquires Rockset
description: Real-time analytics database for data indexing and querying. Powers retrieval infrastructure across OpenAI products.

name: Synthesia 2.0 Platform Launch
description: Synthesia launches Synthesia 2.0, a major platform update transforming it from a video generation tool into a full AI video communications platform with enhanced avatar and editing capabilities.

name: EU AI Act Published in Official Journal
description: The EU AI Act is published in the Official Journal of the European Union, establishing the formal legal text.

name: GPT-4o Mini Released
description: OpenAI releases GPT-4o mini, a cost-efficient model in the 4o family offering strong performance at lower cost for broader adoption.

name: Llama 3.1 Released (8B, 70B, 405B)
description: Meta releases Llama 3.1 including the 405B parameter model, positioned as a frontier-level open model competing with proprietary alternatives.

name: AlphaProof and AlphaGeometry 2: Silver-Medal Math Olympiad Performance
description: Google DeepMind's AlphaProof and AlphaGeometry 2 together solved 4 out of 6 problems at the 2024 International Mathematical Olympiad — the first time AI achieved silver-medal-equivalent performance.

sources.title: AlphaProof and AlphaGeometry 2: Silver-Medal Math Olympiad Performance

name: Meta AI Studio Announced
description: Meta introduces AI Studio for creators and users to build and share custom AI characters, built with Llama 3.1.

sources.title: Create Your Own Custom AI With AI Studio

name: SAM 2 (Segment Anything Model 2) Released
description: Meta releases SAM 2, expanding promptable segmentation to video and enabling real-time object tracking across frames.

sources.title: Introducing SAM 2: The next generation of Meta Segment Anything Model

name: Black Forest Labs Launches with FLUX.1
description: Black Forest Labs launches publicly and releases the FLUX.1 suite of text-to-image models, founded by the original creators of Stable Diffusion and offering state-of-the-art open image generation.

name: EU AI Act Enters into Force
description: The EU AI Act officially enters into force, beginning its staggered application timelines for different AI risk categories.

name: Artifacts Generally Available
description: Anthropic rolls out Artifacts broadly across all Claude plans and mobile apps, allowing Claude to create and display interactive content alongside conversations.

name: o1-preview and o1-mini Released
description: OpenAI introduces the o-series reasoning models with o1-preview and o1-mini, marking the beginning of explicit chain-of-thought reasoning capabilities.

name: Llama 3.2 Released (Vision + Edge Models)
description: Meta releases Llama 3.2 with vision-capable models (11B, 90B) and lightweight text-only models (1B, 3B) for edge and mobile deployment.

sources.title: Llama 3.2: Revolutionizing edge AI and vision

name: Ray-Ban Meta Glasses AI Features
description: Meta announces new AI features for Ray-Ban Meta glasses including memory/recall-style help and live translation capabilities.

sources.title: Ray-Ban Meta Glasses Are Getting New AI Features and Partner Integrations

name: California SB 1047 (Frontier AI Safety) Vetoed
description: California Governor vetoes SB 1047, the frontier AI safety bill, preventing it from becoming law amid debate over AI regulation approaches.

name: Bolt.new Launches
description: StackBlitz launches Bolt.new, an AI-powered full-stack web app builder that enables users to prompt, run, edit, and deploy applications directly in the browser, quickly reaching $40M ARR.

name: Upgraded Claude 3.5 Sonnet + Claude 3.5 Haiku + Computer Use
description: Anthropic releases an upgraded Claude 3.5 Sonnet, new Claude 3.5 Haiku, and introduces computer use capability in beta for agent-style desktop interaction.

sources.title: Introducing computer use, a new Claude 3.5 Sonnet, and Claude 3.5 Haiku

name: Analysis Tool in Claude.ai
description: Anthropic introduces the analysis tool in Claude.ai, allowing Claude to write and run JavaScript for data analysis within conversations.

sources.title: Introducing the analysis tool in Claude.ai

name: Apple Intelligence First Features Available
description: Apple Intelligence first features become available with iOS 18.1, iPadOS 18.1, and macOS 15.1, including writing tools, notification summaries, and Clean Up in Photos.

sources.title: Apple Intelligence is available today on iPhone, iPad, and Mac

name: Claude Desktop Apps (Mac/Windows Beta)
description: Anthropic launches Claude desktop applications for Mac and Windows in public beta, expanding access beyond the web interface.

sources.title: Claude gets desktop apps and dictation support

name: Anthropic–Palantir US Government Partnership
description: Anthropic and Palantir partner to bring Claude AI models to AWS for US government intelligence and defense operations.

sources.title: Anthropic and Palantir Partner for US Gov AI

name: Lovable Web App Launch
description: Lovable launches its AI-powered web app builder, enabling users to create full-stack applications through natural language prompts with automatic deployment and rapid iteration.

name: Amazon–Anthropic Partnership Expanded to $8B
description: Amazon invests an additional $4B in Anthropic, bringing total investment to $8B. AWS confirmed as primary cloud and training partner.

sources.title: Powering the next generation of AI with AWS

name: Model Context Protocol (MCP) Open-Sourced
description: Anthropic open-sources the Model Context Protocol (MCP), a standard for connecting AI assistants to tools and data sources, enabling extensible AI applications.

name: Llama 3.3 70B Released
description: Meta releases Llama 3.3 70B, a text-only model positioned as significantly more efficient while approaching top-tier performance.

name: Sora System Card Published
description: OpenAI publishes the Sora system card documenting safety evaluation and system documentation for its video generation model.

name: Brazil AI Bill Approved by Senate
description: Brazil's Senate approves Bill 2338/2023, a major AI regulatory bill, which moves to the Chamber of Deputies for further consideration.

sources.title: Brazil AI Act - Bill 2338/2023 approved by Senate

name: Gemini 2.0 (Flash Experimental)
description: Google begins public rollout of the Gemini 2.0 family via experimental releases, introducing the next generation of its multimodal AI models.

sources.title: Introducing Gemini 2.0: our new AI model for the agentic era

name: NotebookLM Plus Introduced
description: Google introduces NotebookLM Plus as a premium tier with audio interactivity features and major product upgrades.

sources.title: NotebookLM gets a new look, audio interactivity and a premium version

name: Google Veo 2 Video Generation Model
description: Google DeepMind released Veo 2, delivering state-of-the-art video generation at up to 4K resolution with improved physics simulation and cinematographic controls, outperforming all competing models including Sora.

name: Meta 'Built with Llama' Year Recap
description: Meta recaps the year's Llama trajectory including Llama 3.2 and 3.3, positioning open-source AI models as its core strategy.

sources.title: The future of AI: Built with Llama

name: OpenAI o3 Announced — Benchmark-Shattering Reasoning Model
description: OpenAI announced o3, scoring 96.7% on AIME, 87.7% on GPQA Diamond, and 25.2% on FrontierMath — far surpassing every prior model and approaching expert human performance on the world's hardest benchmarks.

sources.title: OpenAI o3 Announced — Benchmark-Shattering Reasoning Model

name: South Korea AI Basic Act Passed
description: South Korea's National Assembly passes the AI Basic Act, establishing a comprehensive national framework for AI governance.

sources.title: South Korea Artificial Intelligence (AI) Basic Act

name: DeepSeek-V3 Released
description: Chinese AI lab DeepSeek released DeepSeek-V3, a 671B parameter MoE model trained for approximately $5.58M — a fraction of US competitor costs — outperforming Claude 3.5 Sonnet on coding benchmarks.

name: Canada Bill C-27 (AIDA) Dies
description: Canada's Bill C-27, which included the Artificial Intelligence and Data Act (AIDA), dies when Parliament is prorogued without enactment.

name: DeepSeek Chatbot App Launch
description: DeepSeek launches its AI chatbot app on iOS and Android based on the R1 reasoning model, rapidly topping app store charts and sparking global discussion about Chinese AI capabilities and cost efficiency.

name: DeepSeek-R1 Open-Source Reasoning Model
description: DeepSeek released R1, an open-weight reasoning model matching OpenAI's o1 on math, code, and logic benchmarks. Its MIT-licensed release wiped $593B from NVIDIA's valuation and accelerated open-source AI adoption worldwide.

name: The Stargate Project: $500B US AI Infrastructure Initiative
description: President Trump, alongside OpenAI, SoftBank, and Oracle, announced The Stargate Project — a $500 billion commitment over four years to build AI infrastructure in the United States, the largest single AI infrastructure commitment in history.

sources.title: The Stargate Project: $500B US AI Infrastructure Initiative

name: Perplexity Assistant for Android
description: Perplexity launches its AI assistant for Android, positioning it as a default assistant replacement that can answer questions, make calls, and control device functions.

sources.title: Perplexity debuts AI assistant on Android to challenge Alexa, ChatGPT

name: US Executive Order 14110 (AI Safety) Revoked
description: A new executive order explicitly revokes EO 14110 on AI safety and initiates review/rescission of actions taken under it, signaling a shift in US AI policy.

sources.title: Removing Barriers to American Leadership in Artificial Intelligence

name: OpenAI Operator: Autonomous Web-Browsing AI Agent
description: OpenAI launched Operator, a general-purpose AI agent capable of browsing the web and autonomously executing tasks — booking travel, making reservations, and completing shopping — the most capable publicly available AI agent at launch.

sources.title: OpenAI Operator: Autonomous Web-Browsing AI Agent

name: EU AI Liability Directive Withdrawal Announced
description: The European Commission work programme indicates the AI Liability Directive (AILD) proposal will be withdrawn due to lack of foreseeable agreement.

name: Deep Research Feature in ChatGPT
description: OpenAI introduces Deep Research, a feature enabling ChatGPT to conduct comprehensive multi-step research on complex topics.

name: NotebookLM Becomes Workspace Core Service
description: NotebookLM and NotebookLM Plus become Google Workspace core services with enterprise-grade controls and availability.

sources.title: NotebookLM and NotebookLM Plus now available as a Google Workspace core service

name: NotebookLM Plus Available to Individuals
description: Google expands NotebookLM Plus to individual subscribers via Google One AI Premium, beyond enterprise-only availability.

sources.title: Google expands NotebookLM Plus to individual users

name: Grok 3 Released
description: xAI announces and rolls out Grok 3, its flagship model update with significantly improved reasoning and benchmark performance, positioning it as a frontier-level competitor.

name: Figure AI Helix VLA Model for Humanoid Robots
description: Figure AI unveiled Helix, the first Vision-Language-Action model to enable high-rate control of a humanoid robot's entire upper body through a single neural network running on embedded low-power GPUs.

sources.title: Figure AI Helix VLA Model for Humanoid Robots

name: Claude 3.7 Sonnet + Claude Code
description: Anthropic introduces Claude 3.7 Sonnet as a hybrid reasoning model alongside Claude Code, a command-line coding tool for developers.

name: GPT-4.5 (Research Preview)
description: OpenAI introduces GPT-4.5 as a research preview, continuing the GPT-4 series evolution.

title: IBM acquires HashiCorp
description: Multi-cloud infrastructure automation (Terraform, Vault). Foundational layer for hybrid cloud and generative AI deployment at enterprise scale.

name: Runway Gen-4 Released
description: Runway releases Gen-4, its next-generation AI video model with dramatically improved character consistency, scene coherence, and cinematic quality for AI filmmaking.

sources.title: Runway says its latest AI video model is Gen-4

name: Manus AI Agent Launch
description: Manus AI agent officially launches, capable of autonomously executing complex multi-step tasks across the web, generating significant attention as a general-purpose AI agent.

name: New Tools for Building Agents
description: OpenAI announces new agent-oriented tools for developers, expanding the platform's capabilities for building autonomous AI agents.

title: Google acquires Wiz for $32B
description: Cloud security platform. Google's largest acquisition ever. All-cash deal to protect cloud-native applications in the AI era. Wiz crossed $1B ARR in 2025.

name: Web Search in Claude
description: Anthropic introduces web search capabilities in Claude, allowing the assistant to access and reference current information from the internet.

sources.title: Claude can now search the web

name: Next-Generation Audio Models in API
description: OpenAI introduces new STT, TTS, and audio model capabilities for the API, advancing voice and audio AI applications.

name: 4o Image Generation
description: OpenAI integrates image generation capabilities directly into GPT-4o, enabling native multimodal image creation within the model.

name: Gemini 2.5 Introduced (Thinking)
description: Google introduces Gemini 2.5 with 'thinking' capabilities, representing a major advancement in reasoning and developer/app availability.

sources.title: Gemini 2.5: Our most intelligent AI model

name: Midjourney v7 Released
description: Midjourney releases version 7, its most advanced image generation model with significantly improved photorealism, text rendering, and prompt understanding.

name: Llama 4 Released (Scout + Maverick)
description: Meta releases Llama 4 models (Scout and Maverick) as natively multimodal open-weight models, with the larger Behemoth model previewed as a training foundation.

name: Claude Max Plan Introduced
description: Anthropic launches the Max plan offering expanded usage limits and priority access for power users.

name: GPT-4.1 / 4.1 mini / 4.1 nano (API)
description: OpenAI introduces the GPT-4.1 model family for the API with large context support, offering multiple sizes for different use cases.

title: Hugging Face acquires Pollen Robotics
description: French humanoid robotics startup (Reachy 2 robot). Merging generative AI foundation models with physical robotics / embodied AI.

name: o3 + o4-mini Released
description: OpenAI releases o3 and o4-mini, the next step in its o-series reasoning models with tool-using capabilities.

name: First LlamaCon + Meta AI App Launched
description: Meta holds its first LlamaCon developer event and launches the standalone Meta AI app with a Discover feed for accessing AI capabilities.

sources.title: Everything we announced at our first-ever LlamaCon

name: Integrations (Remote MCP Servers)
description: Anthropic introduces integrations to connect Claude with remote MCP servers, expanding beyond local-only desktop MCP connections.

sources.title: Claude can now connect to your world

title: Databricks acquires Neon for $1B
description: Serverless Postgres database. 80% of new databases on Neon were being created automatically by AI agents — signaling need for AI-native database infrastructure.

name: Codex (Cloud Software Engineering Agent)
description: OpenAI introduces Codex as an agent product powered by codex-1, a cloud-based software engineering agent for autonomous coding tasks.

name: NotebookLM Mobile Apps (Android + iOS)
description: Google releases standalone NotebookLM apps for Android and iOS, expanding access to its AI notebook tool on mobile platforms.

name: Flow AI Filmmaking Tool Introduced
description: Google introduces Flow, an AI filmmaking tool built around Veo and related generation/editing workflows for video creation.

sources.title: Meet Flow: AI-powered filmmaking with Veo 3

name: Stitch UI Design Tool Introduced
description: Google Labs introduces Stitch, a UI design tool that generates UI designs and front-end code from text and images.

sources.title: From idea to app: Introducing Stitch, a new way to design UIs

name: Veo 3 + Imagen 4 + Lyria 2 on Vertex AI
description: Google releases Veo 3, Imagen 4, and Lyria 2 on Vertex AI in public preview, providing the next wave of generative media models for developers.

sources.title: Announcing Veo 3, Imagen 4, and Lyria 2 on Vertex AI

title: OpenAI acquires io (Jony Ive) for $6.5B
description: AI hardware-software integration. Jony Ive (former Apple design chief) and LoveFrom team to create consumer AI devices. OpenAI's largest acquisition; signals pivot beyond API-only to hardware.

name: Claude 4 (Opus 4 + Sonnet 4)
description: Anthropic announces Claude 4 with Opus 4 and Sonnet 4 models featuring hybrid modes (near-instant and extended thinking), available via subscription plans and API.

name: DeepSeek R1-0528 Released
description: DeepSeek releases R1-0528 under MIT License, a major reasoning model update that demonstrates frontier-level performance at dramatically lower training costs, intensifying competition with Western AI labs.

title: Meta takes 49% stake in Scale AI for $14.3B
description: 49% non-voting stake in Scale AI; hired founder Alexandr Wang to lead Meta's new superintelligence lab. Securing high-quality training data. Meta's largest outside investment ever.

name: Anthropic–US Department of Defense $200M Agreement
description: Formal DoD contract for Anthropic to advance responsible AI in defense operations across workflows and partners.

sources.title: Anthropic and the Department of Defense

title: Cognition acquires Windsurf
description: AI coding IDE with $82M ARR, 350+ enterprise customers. Acquired days after Google hired Windsurf's CEO/founders in $2.4B reverse acqui-hire.

title: Amazon acquires Bee AI wearable
description: AI wearable startup ($50 bracelet recording conversations in real-time). Competitive response to OpenAI's consumer AI hardware ambitions; integrated with Alexa+.

name: Opal AI Mini-App Builder Introduced
description: Google Labs introduces Opal, an experimental tool for building, editing, and sharing AI mini-apps via natural language and visual workflow editing.

sources.title: Introducing Opal: describe, create, and share your AI mini-apps

name: gpt-oss Open-Weight Models
description: OpenAI introduces gpt-oss-120b and gpt-oss-20b as its first open-weight models, marking a significant shift in OpenAI's approach to model distribution.

name: GPT-5 Released
description: OpenAI introduces GPT-5, its next-generation flagship model representing a major leap in AI capabilities.

name: Imagen 4 Family GA + Imagen 4 Fast
description: Google makes the Imagen 4 family generally available in the Gemini API and introduces the Imagen 4 Fast variant for faster generation.

sources.title: Announcing Imagen 4 Fast and the general availability of the Imagen 4 family in the Gemini API

title: Databricks acquires Tecton
description: Real-time feature store for autonomous AI agents. Sub-100ms feature data serving. Paired with Neon acquisition for complete AI data infrastructure stack.

name: Realtime API Updates (gpt-realtime)
description: OpenAI introduces updates to the Realtime API with gpt-realtime capabilities for real-time AI interaction.

name: Claude Sonnet 4.5 Released
description: Anthropic releases Claude Sonnet 4.5, positioned as a major step forward for coding, agents, and computer use capabilities.

name: Sora 2 Released
description: OpenAI introduces Sora 2, its next-generation video generation model with improved quality and capabilities.

name: Apps in ChatGPT + Apps SDK
description: OpenAI introduces Apps in ChatGPT and the Apps SDK, enabling developers to build applications that integrate directly within the ChatGPT experience.

sources.title: Introducing apps in ChatGPT and the new Apps SDK

name: EU AI Liability Directive Officially Withdrawn
description: The EU AI Liability Directive (AILD) proposal is formally withdrawn, with notice of withdrawal published in the EU Official Journal.

sources.title: European Commission withdraws AI Liability Directive

name: Claude Code on the Web
description: Anthropic introduces Claude Code on the web, enabling browser-based coding workflows without local installation.

name: Microsoft–OpenAI Next Chapter Partnership
description: Updated partnership terms broaden how OpenAI can serve US government national security customers across cloud providers.

sources.title: The next chapter of the Microsoft-OpenAI partnership

name: GPT-5.1 Released
description: OpenAI introduces GPT-5.1, continuing rapid iteration on its GPT-5 series of models.

name: Gemini 3 Pro Preview Released
description: Google begins the Gemini 3 series with the release of Gemini 3 Pro Preview, marking the next generation of Google's AI models.

title: Anthropic acquires Bun
description: JavaScript runtime (7M+ monthly downloads, 82K+ GitHub stars). Infrastructure powering Claude Code, which hit $1B run-rate revenue in 6 months. Bun remains open source.

title: IBM acquires Confluent for $11B
description: Real-time data streaming for enterprise agentic AI. IBM's third major 2025 acquisition ($27.8B total). Sub-100ms data for autonomous agents.

name: GPT-5.2 Released
description: OpenAI introduces GPT-5.2, continuing the rapid advancement of its flagship model series.

name: Google DeepMind–UK Government AI Partnership
description: Expanded partnership with the UK government and deeper collaboration with the UK AI Security Institute on safety and security research.

sources.title: Our partnership with the UK government

name: New ChatGPT Images Experience
description: OpenAI introduces a new ChatGPT Images experience with enhanced image generation and editing capabilities.

sources.title: The new ChatGPT Images is here

name: GPT-5.2-Codex Released
description: OpenAI introduces GPT-5.2-Codex, a specialized coding variant of the GPT-5.2 model optimized for software engineering tasks.

name: Meta Acquires Manus
description: Meta announces the acquisition of Manus AI agent for a reported $2-3 billion valuation, integrating autonomous AI agent capabilities into Meta's product ecosystem.

sources.title: Meta to acquire Chinese startup Manus to boost advanced AI features

title: Meta acquires Manus for ~$2B
description: Singapore-based autonomous AI agent startup with $100M+ ARR in 8 months. Integrating AI agent capabilities into Meta's product ecosystem.

name: South Korea AI Basic Act Takes Effect
description: South Korea implements its AI Basic Act, establishing comprehensive regulations for AI development and deployment with associated grace periods.

sources.title: South Korea launches landmark laws to regulate AI

name: Clawdbot (OpenClaw) Open-Source AI Agent Goes Viral
description: Austrian developer Peter Steinberger released Clawdbot/OpenClaw on GitHub — an open-source AI agent that executes real-world tasks via messaging apps with persistent memory. It reached 60,000+ stars within days, was endorsed by Andrej Karpathy, and led to Steinberger joining OpenAI.

sources.title: Clawdbot (OpenClaw) Open-Source AI Agent Goes Viral

name: Cowork Research Preview
description: Anthropic introduces Cowork as Claude Code for broader work beyond coding, available as a research preview.

sources.title: Cowork: Claude Code for the rest of your work

name: ChatGPT Go Worldwide
description: OpenAI announces ChatGPT Go as available worldwide, expanding access to a lightweight version of ChatGPT.

name: Claude's New Constitution Published
description: Anthropic publishes a new constitution describing Claude's intended values and behavior, advancing transparency in AI alignment.

title: Apple acquires Q.ai for ~$2B
description: Israeli AI startup analyzing facial expressions to understand silent speech. Apple's second-largest acquisition ever. Enhances Siri with non-verbal, camera-based interaction.

name: Colorado AI Consumer Protection (SB24-205) Takes Effect
description: Colorado's high-risk AI consumer protection obligations take effect, requiring businesses to assess and mitigate algorithmic discrimination.

sources.title: SB24-205 Consumer Protections for Artificial Intelligence

name: Claude Opus 4.6 Released
description: Anthropic releases Claude Opus 4.6, its flagship Opus model upgrade with expanded capabilities.

name: Claude Enterprise Self-Serve
description: Anthropic makes Claude Enterprise purchasable self-serve without requiring a sales conversation, expanding enterprise adoption.

name: Claude Sonnet 4.6 Released
description: Anthropic releases Claude Sonnet 4.6 with 1M token context in beta and broad capability upgrades across the model.

name: ProducerAI Joins Google Labs
description: ProducerAI becomes a Google Labs experiment for AI-assisted music creation, expanding Google's creative AI tools.

sources.title: ProducerAI: Your music creation partner, now in Google Labs

name: Anthropic Acquires Vercept
description: AI startup advancing computer use capabilities. Vercept built tools for complex agentic tasks including Vy, a cloud-based agent operating a remote MacBook.

sources.title: Anthropic acquires Vercept to advance Claude's computer use capabilities

title: Anthropic acquires Vercept

name: GPT-5.4 (Thinking + Pro)
description: OpenAI introduces GPT-5.4 with Thinking capabilities and a Pro variant, representing the latest iteration of its flagship model series.

name: OpenAI–Department of War Agreement
description: OpenAI publishes agreement describing guardrails for how its AI systems would be deployed for defense and government use cases.

sources.title: Our agreement with the Department of War

name: Code Review for Claude Code
description: Anthropic introduces code review capabilities for Claude Code, enabling automated code review within the developer workflow.

name: GPT-5.1 Retired in ChatGPT
description: OpenAI removes GPT-5.1 models from ChatGPT availability as part of model migration to newer versions.

name: Interactive Visuals in Claude Conversations
description: Anthropic introduces the ability for Claude to create interactive charts, diagrams, and visualizations directly within conversations.

sources.title: Claude builds interactive visuals right in your conversation

name: Ask Maps (Gemini in Google Maps)
description: Google rolls out Ask Maps, a conversational Gemini-powered experience in Google Maps, initially in the US and India on mobile.

sources.title: Google overhauls its Maps app, adding in more AI features

name: Meta Delays 'Avocado' Flagship Model
description: Reports indicate Meta pushed the rollout of its next flagship model 'Avocado' to at least May 2026 due to performance concerns.

sources.title: Meta delays rollout new AI model 'Avocado'

name: 1M Context GA for Opus 4.6 and Sonnet 4.6
description: Anthropic makes 1M token context generally available for both Opus 4.6 and Sonnet 4.6 with standard pricing across the full context window.

sources.title: 1M context is now generally available for Opus 4.6 and Sonnet 4.6

name: OpenAI–AWS US Defense AI Distribution Deal
description: OpenAI partners with AWS to sell AI to US defense and government agencies through classified and unclassified cloud environments.

sources.title: OpenAI to sell AI to US agencies through Amazon cloud unit

title: OpenAI acquires Astral
description: Open-source Python tool-maker (uv, Ruff, ty). Integrating into Codex team to broaden AI capabilities throughout the software development lifecycle.
